_cite.reason: URL-normalization 1c: strip trailing markdown-emphasis `*`/backtick glued to citation URLs in intelligence_item_sections.content_md (customer-checkable link + validate_item_provenance criterion-2 exact-compare). Operator ruling 2026-07-04. Same transform as the write-site fix.

prior.content_md:
**Right to Repair — Operational Preparation:**

1. **Returns and Reverse-Logistics Infrastructure:** The manufacturer repair obligation (§§ 479a–479g BGB-E) applies from the law's entry into force regardless of when goods were purchased. For the workspace handling consumer electronics, white goods, or other Annex II products in DE/EU lanes, this will generate an increase in post-warranty returns flows for repair routing. The workspace should assess whether current returns-handling SLAs, warehouse dwell-time allocations, and customs re-import procedures for repaired goods are fit for purpose by 31 July 2026. Gap from baseline: reverse-logistics volumes may increase materially for electronics and appliance verticals.

2. **Tracking and Documentation Systems:** Sellers will be required to inform consumers of the repair vs. replacement choice and document the decision before providing a remedy. Where the workspace issues delivery or returns labels, its operational systems should be capable of flagging covered product categories (Annex II) and routing items to manufacturer-designated repair facilities vs. replacement stock. Data fields to capture: Annex II product category, manufacturer, model/serial, consumer purchase date, repair/replace decision, warranty-extension trigger. Deadline: 31 July 2026 entry into force.

3. **Spare-Parts Distribution Lane Assessment:** Manufacturers of covered product groups must supply spare parts, tools, and repair information and must not use software or technical measures that hinder repair. This is expected to generate new or expanded spare-parts import lanes into DE (from Asia-Pacific manufacturing origins), creating opportunities in the workspace's air and road freight verticals. The workspace should engage manufacturer clients now on spare-parts forecasting and bonded-warehouse requirements. No regulatory deadline — commercial readiness window: Q3 2026.

4. **Contract Clause Updates:** Existing logistics service agreements with manufacturers of Annex II goods should be reviewed for clauses addressing: (a) liability allocation for returned/repaired goods; (b) handling of goods where repairability is now a quality standard under §434(3) BGB-E (goods that cannot be repaired may be classified as defective); (c) cross-border warranty-extension documentation requirements. Legal confirmation of role classification (logistics intermediary vs. distributor/importer) is required before 31 July 2026.

5. **Training:** Commercial and compliance teams handling DE/EU consumer-goods accounts should be briefed on the new seller pre-remedy information obligation and the warranty-extension mechanics. Training scope: sales, operations, and customs teams. Deadline: prior to law entry into force, target June 2026.

**Medical Registry Law — Operational Preparation:**

For workspaces handling implant logistics or pharmaceutical sample shipments in DE: confirm with clients whether new registry-linkage pseudonymisation requirements affect shipping manifest data fields. The existing Implantateregistergesetz is directly amended by the bill. Gap from baseline: likely minimal for most workspace profiles; confirm with healthcare-vertical clients. Deadline: upon law entry into force (date not yet confirmed — pending Bundestag vote).

*Source: Freshfields Risk & Compliance Blog, 'Repair instead of replace', Feb 2026. https://www.freshfields.com/en/our-thinking/blogs/risk-and-compliance/repair-instead-of-replace-germany-moves-to-implement-the-eu-right-to-repair-dire-102mgn4; Cleary Gottlieb publication, 'German Draft Bill Implementing Directive (EU) 2024/1799', Mar 2026. https://www.clearygottlieb.com/news-and-insights/publication-listing/german-draft-bill-implementing-directive-eu-2024-1799*
